based_on: Structurally derived from the same step 1 receipt design as fixtures/drafts/mazur.golden.draft.jsonl (not yet committed at the time of this fixture's authoring).

what_was_NOT_mutated: optimizer.factors[*].value (product of factors still equals the original gradient)
updates[4].update (still derived from original gradient, so it will cascade to Rule 5 failure when Rule 5 is implemented)
updates[4].weight_after (still derived from original update, cascade to Rule 6 when implemented)
all other parameter updates' factor values

single_target_invariant: Reconciliation produces exactly one Rule 4 failure, and that failure names parameter w5. No other parameter (w1, w2, w3, w4, w6, w7, w8) may appear in the failure list under the current numeric_policy.tolerance = 1e-9. If a future tolerance change or fixture edit causes other parameters to also surface, the anti-circularity gate is muddied and the tests must fail until the fixture or policy is re-aligned.

precision_normalization_history.issue: Initial hand-derivation of the non-mutated update records used 9-sig-fig values for gradient / update / weight_after. For w6 and w8 specifically, the IEEE-754 product of the stored factor doubles differs from the 9-sig-fig stored gradient by ~3e-9 and ~1.5e-9 — both above the 1e-9 absolute tolerance. This produced incidental Rule 4 failures on w6 and w8 alongside the deliberate w5 failure.
precision_normalization_history.fix: Replaced w6 and w8 gradient / update / weight_after fields with full-double-precision values that round-trip exactly through JSON.parse. This is acceptable because the bad fixture is a contract-test artifact, not a canonical receipt. The eventual mazur.golden.jsonl (engine-emitted) will use 9-sig-fig canonical emission under numeric_policy.byte_output.
precision_normalization_history.v0_2_followup: Tolerance policy should likely become hybrid (rtol + atol) rather than pure-absolute 1e-9. Production numerical test suites (PyTorch, numpy, JAX) all use |a-b| <= max(atol, rtol * max(|a|,|b|)). Investigation issue is the natural way to track this.

cascading_failures_expected_when_other_r: Rule 5: update == learning_rate * gradient (cascade from w5; stored update -0.041083520 was derived from the original gradient, so it is no longer consistent with the mutated stored gradient -0.082166041)
Rule 6: weight_after == weight_before + update (cascade from w5 if the reconciler recomputes update from the mutated gradient; if it uses the stored update value, Rule 6 passes independently)
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures. The post_update_forward.status = 'pending_engine_first_run' on this fixture must NOT mask the Rule 4 failure. If the reconciler reports 'draft not promoted' before reporting Rule 4, the failure-priority rule is violated.
error_message_format_note: Delta and tolerance render via toExponential(9) in src/bin/bp.ts (scientific notation permitted for error magnitudes), not src/format.ts (receipt emission, plain decimal only). Error-format and receipt-format are separate code paths.
purpose: Proves the verifier checks math, not just byte equality and schema validity. Written and committed BEFORE the engine produces any receipt, so the verifier cannot be co-developed against engine output. Implements 'bad receipts precede good receipts' from the law stack.
post_v0_1_extensions: v0.2 should add one bad fixture per reconciliation rule (target = eight bad fixtures total, one per rule). v0.1 only requires the Rule 4 fixture as the minimum anti-circularity gate. Also v0.2: revisit numeric_policy.tolerance to a hybrid rtol+atol form per Agent A research findings (see precision_normalization_history.v0_2_followup).
